name: Anthropic Claude API
description: First-party API for Claude models; reference for tool use, prompt caching, MCP, extended thinking

provider.comment: Anthropic trains and serves the Claude models over its own RESTful API at https://api.anthropic.com.

category.comment: Vendor that trains and serves its own Claude models over its own API.

api-launched.comment: Approximate. Anthropic first offered limited API access in early 2023; broad/self-serve availability of the Claude developer API (Console) landed around mid-2023 alongside Claude 2. Exact GA month is not pinned by a single primary source.

status.comment: Core Messages/Batches/Token-Counting/Models APIs are GA; some newer surfaces (Files, Skills, Agents/Sessions) are beta as of the 2026-07-24 check.

auth-methods.comment: Requests use an x-api-key header from Console, or an OAuth Bearer token obtained via Workload Identity Federation (POST /v1/oauth/token). Cloud-platform access (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry) uses the provider's IAM instead.

self-serve-signup.comment: Any developer can create a Claude Console account and generate an API key without sales contact.

free-credits-trial.comment: Pricing FAQ: new users receive a small amount of free credits to test the API; extended enterprise trials via sales.

regional-endpoints.comment: The first-party Claude API is global by default (single endpoint). Regional/multi-region routing is offered via the partner cloud platforms; the first-party API adds a US inference option through inference_geo.

data-residency-options.comment: For Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6 and later, inference_geo:"us" pins inference to the US (1.1x pricing multiplier) as a first-party data-residency option; default is global.

model-breadth.comment: Only Anthropic's own Claude model family is served through this API.

open-weight-models.comment: Claude models are proprietary; no open-weight models are offered.

model-version-pinning.comment: Every Claude model ID is a pinned snapshot; dated IDs (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) and the newer dateless IDs both resolve to fixed releases.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: The first-party Claude API does not offer self-serve fine-tuning or private custom-model deployment; Claude fine-tuning has historically been offered only through partner platforms such as Amazon Bedrock.

model-catalog-size.comment: As of 2026-07-24 roughly a dozen callable Claude models: current Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5, Haiku 4.5 plus several legacy Opus/Sonnet/Haiku snapshots (Mythos 5 is invite-only).

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Messages API (POST /v1/messages) is the primary multi-turn chat endpoint.

streaming.comment: Token streaming (SSE) is supported; the SDKs and stream field expose incremental responses.

tool-function-calling.comment: Client-defined tools via the tools parameter with input_schema; Claude returns structured tool_use blocks.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Parallel tool use is the default; it can be disabled with tool_choice.disable_parallel_tool_use.

structured-output.comment: Native Structured Outputs plus strict tool use (strict: true) guarantee server-enforced schema conformance.

system-prompt.comment: A dedicated top-level system field is supported (a single initial system message).

image-input-vision.comment: All current Claude models support text and image input and vision.

audio-input.comment: Audio input is not supported; audio content is ignored/stripped from input.

document-pdf-input.comment: Native PDF processing is a first-class Claude API feature (document content blocks / PDF support).

image-generation.comment: Claude models output text only; no image-generation endpoint is offered.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: No speech/audio synthesis output endpoint; models produce text output only.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Anthropic does not offer a first-party text-embeddings endpoint; it has historically directed developers to third-party providers (e.g. Voyage AI).

prompt-caching.comment: Both automatic and explicit-breakpoint prompt caching via cache_control; cache reads cost 0.1x base input, 5m writes 1.25x, 1h writes 2x.

batch-api.comment: Message Batches API (POST /v1/messages/batches) processes bulk requests asynchronously at a 50% discount on input and output tokens.

max-context-window.comment: 1M-token context on Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, and Sonnet 5 (and Opus 4.6/4.7, Sonnet 4.6) as of 2026-07-24; Haiku 4.5 is 200k.

extended-reasoning.comment: API-level extended thinking (thinking.type: enabled with budget_tokens), adaptive thinking, and an effort control expose reasoning depth.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party server-side web_search tool executed on Anthropic infrastructure ($10 per 1,000 searches); a web_fetch tool is also provided.

built-in-code-execution.comment: First-party server-side code_execution tool runs Python/bash in an Anthropic-hosted sandbox; 1,550 free container-hours/month then $0.05/hour.

mcp-support.comment: MCP connector lets the Messages API connect to remote MCP servers without a separate MCP client.

files-retrieval-api.comment: Files API (beta) uploads and manages files referenced across multiple API calls; no fully managed vector-retrieval/RAG service beyond this.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Claude Managed Agents exposes stateful Agents, Sessions, and Environments APIs (beta) above raw inference.

openai-compatible-api.comment: An OpenAI-Chat-Completions compatibility layer works with the OpenAI SDK via a base-URL swap to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/, but Anthropic positions it for testing rather than production and it drops some features (prompt caching, strict schema, audio).

official-sdks.comment: Official client SDKs: Python, TypeScript, C# (.NET), Go, Java, PHP, and Ruby. No first-party Rust SDK.

native-api-style.comment: Native request/response shape is the Anthropic Messages format (content blocks, tool_use/tool_result).

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Batch job completion is retrieved by polling the Batches API rather than via webhooks/callbacks; no async callback mechanism is documented for the core API.

published-sla.comment: No public contractual uptime SLA figure; enterprise custom terms (custom rate limits, dedicated support) are negotiated via sales. A public status page exists but is best-effort, not an SLA.

rate-limit-model.comment: Fixed usage tiers (Start/Build/Scale) with per-tier RPM/TPM and spend caps; organizations auto-advance and can request increases, with custom limits above Scale via sales.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Single-vendor first-party API; no cross-provider failover/routing (a gateway feature).

status-page.comment: Public status page at status.claude.com (formerly status.anthropic.com) tracks Claude API (api.anthropic.com), Console, and other components.

no-training-default.comment: "By default, we will not use your inputs or outputs from our commercial products (e.g. Claude for Work, Anthropic API, Claude Gov, etc.) to train our models." Training only occurs if a customer explicitly opts in or submits feedback.

zero-data-retention.comment: Zero-data-retention is available to approved commercial Claude API / Claude Code Enterprise customers on a per-organization basis (Anthropic does not store inputs/outputs except as needed for law/misuse; safety-classifier results are still retained). Standard default retention is otherwise 30+ days.

compliance-certifications.comment: Certifications for commercial products (incl. the Anthropic API): SOC 2 Type I & II, ISO 27001:2022, ISO/IEC 42001:2023 (no matching tag), and a HIPAA-ready configuration with BAA available. GDPR/FedRAMP/PCI are not listed as certifications.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Claude Console supports organization workspaces and access controls; enterprise SSO/SAML is offered for console/org governance (also Workload Identity Federation for API auth).

pricing-model.comment: Per-token usage billing (per million input/output tokens), postpaid on actual monthly usage; no first-party provisioned-throughput or subscription tier for the API.

unified-billing.comment: A single Anthropic account/bill covers all reachable Claude models (trivially true for a single-vendor API).

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch API gives a 50% discount on input and output; prompt-cache reads are 0.1x base input price. The two discounts stack.

cheapest-entry.comment: Self-serve pay-as-you-go with no minimum commitment; new users also get a small amount of free trial credits.
